core_mechanism: an adaptive, history-dependent bias potential that progressively accumulates in already-visited regions of a low-dimensional coordinate space to flatten the effective landscape and drive the system out of previously sampled basins, where those reduced coordinates are themselves learned on-the-fly from analysis of the accumulating trajectory
brief_reason: The abstract states acceleration is achieved by constructing a bias potential over a patchwork of locally valid collective coordinates that are obtained from trajectory analyses and adapt to newly encountered features, i.e. self-reinforcing biasing over online-learned reduced coordinates.
